SEA-eco
  • SEA-eco Documentation
  • View page source

SEA-eco Documentation

SEA-eco lives inside the pySEA (Python Simulation Experiment Analysis) namespace package and provides the core Simulation Experiment Analysis (SEA) data model, plotting helpers, and signal processing utilities.

Guides

  • Getting Started
    • Quick usage
    • Building the docs locally
  • Mission and Architecture
    • Mission statement
    • Notes on the pySEA namespace
    • FAIR principles in practice
    • Architectural overview (numpy backend)
    • Architecture for data comparison
  • SEA-eco Architecture (numpy backend)
    • Core building blocks
    • SEA HDF5 layout (*.sea)
    • Useful patterns (mirrors “Basic usage.ipynb”)
    • Dimension-aware comparisons
  • Signal Processing Guide
    • Correlation and alignment
    • Shifting data
    • Maxima search
    • Lower-level primitives
  • Plotting Signals
    • Dimension inference
    • Plot utilities
    • Interactive plotting
  • Colors and Color Wheels
    • 1D colormaps (pySEA.sea_eco._plotting.colors)
    • 2D color wheels (pySEA.sea_eco._plotting.colors2D)
  • Readers and I/O
    • SEA HDF5 (*.sea)
    • Swift / third-party ingest (pySEA.sea_eco.io)
